original_abstract: This study examines the pivotal role of rare earth elements (REEs) in the global economy, focusing specifically on their application in high-performance permanent magnets. These technologies rely heavily on neodymium (Nd), praseodymium (Pr), dysprosium (Dy), and cerium (Ce), with a smaller dependence on gadolinium (Gd). Currently, the magnet industry represents the largest segment of the rare earth market by volume and is projected to maintain its dominance over other sectors. Drawing on market intelligence from Roskill and Wood Mackenzie, this report highlights forecasts indicating that magnet applications will constitute approximately 40% of total REE demand by 2030. Such significant growth suggests a looming supply-demand imbalance for critical magnetic elements. To address these challenges, this paper analyzes the current supply chains and market dynamics across the United States, Europe, Japan, and China.
humanized_abstract: Rare earth elements and their application to high performance permanent magnets This paper briefly discusses the role of the rare earth elements (REEs) in today’s global economy, with focus given to their application to high performance permanent magnets that contain neodymium (Nd), praseodymium (Pr), dysprosium (Dy) and cerium (Ce) with small amounts of gadolinium (Gd). These permanent magnets are primarily used within the motor and subsequently the magnet industry is the largest of the rare earth applications by volume. This paper uses data from industry analyst Roskill and Wood Mackenzie, and uses market analysis to calculate that applications of permanent magnets are forecast to make up some 40% of rare earth element demand in 2030. This article explores the supply chain in the rare earth elements sector for permanent magnets for the following countries: United States of America, Europe, Japan and China.

This paper uses data from industry analyst Roskill and Wood Mackenzie, and uses market analysis to calculate that applications of permanent magnets are forecast to make up some 40% of rare earth element demand in 2030. This article explores the supply chain in the rare earth elements sector for permanent magnets for the following countries: United States of America, Europe, Japan and China.
